gemini_data.category_and_description: Salesforce is a cloud-based software company that specializes in customer relationship management (CRM). Its core offering helps businesses manage customer interactions, sales processes, marketing campaigns, and customer service.
gemini_data.auth_methods: Salesforce supports OAuth 2.0 for API authentication, which is the most common and secure method. It also supports traditional username and password authentication, often with additional security measures like multi-factor authentication. SAML is also supported for single sign-on (SSO).
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Salesforce offers a free Developer Edition account, which provides access to the platform for testing and development purposes without cost. Paid plans are required for production use and advanced features, with API access often being an add-on cost on lower tiers. Some API access is gated and may require a paid plan or sales consultation.
gemini_data.api_surface: Salesforce provides a comprehensive API surface, including a well-documented REST API and a GraphQL API. It also supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP) through Salesforce Hosted MCP Servers, enabling AI agents to interact with Salesforce data and logic.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, Salesforce could be an agent toolkit today. Its robust API surface (REST, GraphQL) and support for the Model Context Protocol (MCP) allow for integration with AI agents. The main blocker, if any, would be the potential cost associated with extensive API usage on paid plans, as API access can be an add-on cost.

gemini_data.category_and_description: HubSpot is a customer relationship management (CRM) platform that offers a suite of tools for marketing, sales, service, and content management, designed to help businesses grow.
gemini_data.auth_methods: HubSpot's APIs support OAuth 2.0 for multi-account integrations and private app access tokens for single-account setups. API Keys have been retired and are no longer supported for new integrations.
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: HubSpot offers a free CRM with basic features, and various paid plans (Starter, Professional, Enterprise) for its different 'Hubs' (Marketing, Sales, Service, Content, Data, Revenue). API access and features are generally tied to these plans, with higher tiers offering more advanced capabilities. Developers can create private apps for free, which provide API access for a single account. For broader distribution or more advanced features, paid plans or specific agreements may be required.
gemini_data.api_surface: HubSpot provides a well-documented REST API. They also have an API reference documentation that is versioned. There is no readily available information indicating the presence of an existing Model Context Protocol (MCP) server.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, HubSpot could be an agent toolkit today. Its comprehensive REST API, extensive documentation, and various authentication methods make it suitable for integration. The main blocker, if any, would be understanding the specific API rate limits and tier-based access to certain APIs, which would require careful planning for robust agent functionality.

gemini_data.api_surface: Clay does not offer a traditional public REST or GraphQL API for direct data access. However, it provides an HTTP API integration that allows programmatic interaction with external APIs, including JWT authentication. It does not appear to have an existing Model Context Protocol (MCP) server.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Clay could be an agent toolkit today, as it provides infrastructure for data acquisition, agentic workflows, and GTM plays. The main blocker for a fully integrated agent toolkit might be the lack of a direct, comprehensive public API for external systems to programmatically control Clay's core functionalities beyond the HTTP API integration for external services. However, its own 'Claygent' AI agents and workflow orchestration capabilities suggest it's already moving in this direction.

gemini_data.category_and_description: GitHub is a web-based platform for version control and collaboration, primarily used by software developers to store, manage, and track code changes, and to collaborate on projects.
gemini_data.auth_methods: GitHub supports multiple authentication methods, including OAuth2, Personal Access Tokens (PATs), GitHub App tokens, and basic authentication (username/password, though discouraged for production).
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: GitHub offers a free tier with unlimited public/private repositories, suitable for individuals and open-source projects. gemini_data.api_surface: GitHub provides both a documented REST API and a GraphQL API. There are also mentions of Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers related to GitHub, particularly in the context of AI agents and GitHub Copilot.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, GitHub could be an agent toolkit today. Its extensive API surface (REST and GraphQL), coupled with various authentication methods and a free tier, makes it accessible for integration. The main blocker for a highly sophisticated agent might be the complexity of managing different authentication models for various use cases and ensuring optimal API rate limit usage, though these are common challenges with large platforms.

gemini_data.category_and_description: Vercel is a cloud platform for frontend developers to build, deploy, and scale web applications and static sites, with a strong focus on performance and developer experience.

Two-factor authentication (2FA) is also available via Authenticator App or Passkey.
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Vercel offers a free 'Hobby' plan for personal projects and early experimentation, allowing developers to get started without payment or sales calls. Paid plans (Pro and Enterprise) are available for production applications and teams, with Pro starting at $20 per developer per month.
gemini_data.api_surface: Vercel provides a documented REST API for programmatic interaction with user accounts, deployments, domains, secrets, and environment variables. It also supports Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, which enable LLMs to communicate with external tools and data sources.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, Vercel could be an agent toolkit today. Vercel has a robust API surface, supports OAuth for authentication, and has a free tier for developers to experiment with. Furthermore, Vercel is actively involved in the AI space with its Model Context Protocol (MCP) and AI SDK, enabling LLMs to interact with Vercel projects and tools. The main blocker would be the complexity of integrating Vercel's specific services and APIs into a general-purpose agent framework, requiring detailed knowledge of Vercel's ecosystem.

gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Gated - Requires a sales call and a separate contract agreement. API access is an add-on to a PitchBook Platform subscription and is priced separately.
gemini_data.api_surface: REST API. Offers various relational endpoints for financing details, deal stock information, and VC exit predictor data. MCP server is not explicitly mentioned.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: No, due to the gated nature and requirement for a sales call and contract. The primary blocker is the lack of self-serve credentials and the need for a paid, negotiated contract.

gemini_data.category_and_description: AI-powered research and writing assistant that helps users organize, summarize, and synthesize information from uploaded sources.
gemini_data.auth_methods: Google Account / OAuth2 (for Enterprise)
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Self-serve (free tier available), with paid tiers and enterprise options.
gemini_data.api_surface: REST API (Enterprise only, documented in Google Cloud docs), unofficial Python wrappers exist.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Limited. The official API is enterprise-only. Unofficial wrappers may be unstable or violate terms of service. An MCP server exists for agent integration.

gemini_data.category_and_description: Otter.ai is a voice-to-text transcription software that uses artificial intelligence to record, transcribe, and summarize meetings and conversations.
gemini_data.auth_methods: Otter.ai's Public API uses Bearer token authentication, which requires an API key generated from the developer dashboard. Client credentials and HTTP Basic Auth are also supported for token generation.
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Otter.ai offers a free Basic plan with limited transcription minutes. Access to the Public API is available for Enterprise customers and requires contacting an account manager to enable access.
gemini_data.api_surface: Otter.ai provides a documented REST API with endpoints for managing conversations, channels, workspaces, and audio downloads. They also support webhooks for real-time notifications and have an existing Model Context Protocol (MCP) server.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, Otter.ai could be an agent toolkit today. The main blocker is that while the API is documented and supports MCP, access to the full public API is gated for Enterprise customers, requiring contact with an account manager.

gemini_data.category_and_description: AI-powered meeting assistant that records, transcribes, and summarizes video calls.
gemini_data.auth_methods: API Key, OAuth2, SSO (Google, Microsoft)
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Freemium model with free tier for individuals; paid tiers (Premium, Team, Business, Enterprise) for advanced features and team collaboration. Enterprise requires sales contact.
gemini_data.api_surface: REST API, gRPC (via SDKs). Offers endpoints for meetings, summaries, transcripts, highlights, and webhooks. An MCP server is available.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, Fathom can be used as an agent toolkit today. The primary blocker for advanced use cases might be understanding the full scope and limitations of the public API and potential rate limits.

gemini_data.category_and_description: Consensus is an AI-powered search engine for academic research, designed to help users find, understand, and synthesize peer-reviewed literature.
gemini_data.auth_methods: API Key / OAuth2 (implied by institutional access)
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Gated for API access (application required), but has free and paid tiers for individual use.
gemini_data.api_surface: REST API (MCPAPI Reference available). Supports OpenAPI.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, can be used as an agent toolkit. The API provides access to a vast index of research papers and allows for structured output generation.

gemini_data.category_and_description: Document Intelligence Platform - Automates document workflows and extracts structured data from unstructured documents like PDFs, spreadsheets, and scanned files for AI applications.
gemini_data.auth_methods: API Key
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Self-serve with a free account and API key generation. Offers free credits on the Standard plan.
gemini_data.api_surface: REST API. Offers core functions like Parse, Extract, Split, and Edit. Supports SDKs for Python, Node.js, and Go. An MCP server is available for agent tool calling.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, it can be used as an agent toolkit today. The main blocker would be the need to manage API keys and potentially integrate with their SDKs or REST API.

gemini_data.category_and_description: AI Software Engineer - An autonomous AI agent that plans, codes, tests, debugs, and deploys software end-to-end.
gemini_data.auth_methods: Service User API Key, Personal Access Token (PAT)
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Self-serve with Free, Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans. Some plans require sales contact (Enterprise).
gemini_data.api_surface: REST API. Supports Organization and Enterprise API scopes. MCP server exists for programmatic access to platform capabilities.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, Devin can be used as an agent toolkit today. Its API and MCP server allow integration into existing workflows and custom agent development. gemini_data.category_and_description: Category: Media & Entertainment / Data Access. Description: The YouTube Data API allows developers to access and manage YouTube data, including searching for videos, retrieving metadata, managing playlists, and interacting with comments. It can also be used to extract video transcripts.
gemini_data.auth_methods: API Key and OAuth 2.0.
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Self-serve. Developers can obtain an API key for free from the Google Cloud Console. While there are daily quota limits (10,000 units per project), there is no direct cost or paid plan required for basic access. Quota increases can be requested.
gemini_data.api_surface: Yes, there is a documented REST API. There is no indication of a GraphQL API. There are existing Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers for YouTube, such as the YouTube MCP Server, which allow AI language models to interact with YouTube content.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, this could be an agent toolkit today. The main blocker is that the official YouTube Data API v3 has limitations regarding transcript access, specifically only providing access to manual captions and not auto-generated ones, which are available for a much larger number of videos. Dedicated transcript APIs or MCP servers often bridge this gap.

gemini_data.category_and_description: AI-powered meeting notetaker and conversation intelligence platform that records video calls, Slack huddles, and in-person meetings to provide summaries, insights, and coaching feedback.
gemini_data.auth_methods: OAuth2, Personal Access Token (PAT), Workspace Access Token (WAT), JWT, API Key (Secret Key)
gemini_data.self_serve_vs_gated: Free plan available with limitations; paid plans and API access start at the Starter tier ($15/month per user), with full API capabilities and Workspace Access Tokens available on Business and Enterprise plans. Sales call required for Enterprise pricing.
gemini_data.api_surface: REST API. Supports reading meeting data, managing recordings, uploading external recordings, and receiving real-time events via webhooks. An MCP server exists for AI agent integrations.
gemini_data.buildability_verdict: Yes, it can be an agent toolkit today. The API provides access to meeting data, transcripts, and AI notes, and the MCP server facilitates AI agent integrations.
